task_completion.visual_evidence: The Mozilla Thunderbird application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate and select the 'Inbox' folder under 'Local Folders'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Inbox' folder under the 'Local Folders' account is visible and/or highlighted in the left-hand folder pane.

task_completion.description: Trigger the compact action for the 'Inbox' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A right-click context menu is open on the 'Inbox' folder with the 'Compact' option highlighted/clicked, OR the 'File' menu is open with 'Compact Folders' selected, OR the status bar at the bottom shows a 'Compacting folder...' progress message.

task_completion.description: Locate and select the 'Junk' folder under 'Local Folders'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Junk' folder under the 'Local Folders' account is visible and/or highlighted in the left-hand folder pane.

task_completion.description: Trigger the compact action for the 'Junk' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A right-click context menu is open on the 'Junk' folder with the 'Compact' option highlighted/clicked, OR the 'File' menu is open with 'Compact Folders' selected, OR the status bar at the bottom shows a 'Compacting folder...' progress message.

task_completion.description: Ensure existing (non-deleted) emails remain accessible in the folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The message list pane shows multiple emails still present and readable in both the 'Inbox' and 'Junk' folders after the compacting actions are performed.

integrity.description: The agent used the Thunderbird GUI to compact the folders.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Thunderbird menus and buttons, rather than terminal windows using command-line tools (like sed, awk, or truncate) to modify the mbox files directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually delete active emails to reduce folder size.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen selecting visible emails in the message list and pressing 'Delete' or clicking the 'Delete' button. The size reduction must come purely from the 'Compact' function.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Thunderbird email client.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches Mozilla Thunderbird, with standard email panes (folder list, message list, message preview), rather than a text editor opening the raw mailbox files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target folders to compact are 'Inbox' and 'Junk' under 'Local Folders'. A successful compaction reduces the underlying file sizes to less than 70% of their original sizes (ratio < 0.7) by purging hidden 'ghost' data. The original active (non-deleted) message counts must remain strictly intact; the agent should only use Thunderbird's built-in 'Compact' function and must not manually delete any visible emails.
